title: aes.rs: define TAG/IV constants as usize not u32
description: AES_GCM_TAG_LEN and AES_GCM_IV_LEN are defined as u32 (matching the C FFI type), but their primary use is in Rust size/length contexts that want usize: [u8; AES_GCM_TAG_LEN as usize], iv.len() != AES_GCM_IV_LEN as usize, etc. This forces as usize at every Rust-side site and as u32 only at the two FFI call sites. The canonical Rust idiom is the opposite: define as usize (natural for sizes), cast to u32 at FFI boundary. Rewrite: const AES_GCM_TAG_LEN: usize = 16; const AES_GCM_IV_LEN: usize = 12; then use AES_GCM_TAG_LEN as u32 at the two wolfhsm_aes_gcm_{en,de}crypt call sites. This also makes [u8; AES_GCM_TAG_LEN] work without a cast in both the return type and tag parameter.
